name: TAO 4h Asymmetric Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: continuation is entered after a reset-and-reclaim sequence, but trigger, risk, and exit roles are kept deliberately separate so winners have room to pay for losers
thesis: Asymmetric continuation is still only worth testing where trend structure can plausibly support it, and that remains TAO 4h. But the last cycle showed the prior version was simply a bad idea, so this version simplifies the trigger into reset-and-reclaim instead of letting a boundary-touch pretend to be continuation. The edge is cleaner role separation plus a structurally earlier continuation location.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h still produces negative asymmetry after a reset-and-reclaim trigger, the continuation family should be abandoned entirely.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price pulls back, reclaims fast structure and trend context, then continues, while exit logic preserves asymmetry by treating trigger, risk, and exit as separate jobs.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: The previous TAO asymmetric family failed despite adequate trade count, which suggests the trigger was bad rather than the management concept itself. Requiring a clearer reset-and-reclaim location should give the same asymmetric exit structure a fairer entry basis.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it is still the only lane in the basket with enough trend structure for continuation asymmetry to make sense.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The reset-and-reclaim may still happen too late in the recovery, turning continuation entries into local exhaustion buys.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is acceptable but winners still decay, shorten the time-based de-risk step before touching the reclaim trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A modest number of continuation attempts, many small wins/losses, and a smaller number of larger winners carrying expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears density and the reset-and-reclaim version materially improves expectancy over the prior failed asymmetric family.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or another clearly negative expectancy profile despite acceptable count.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because reset-and-reclaim should be denser than the prior boundary-touch logic

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit lane for role-separated asymmetric continuation after a cleaner reset-and-reclaim trigger.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough continuation attempts with materially improved asymmetry.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h still produces negative expectancy despite adequate count.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with a few larger winners carrying expectancy.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same reset-and-reclaim continuation survives on the transition-heavy primary lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: DOGE 4h preserves enough continuation structure with the same role separation.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h confirms the concept still needs stronger trend contamination.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: More frequent but smaller asymmetry opportunities than TAO.

test_lanes.why_included: Useful control to test whether pure chop still suppresses the same continuation logic.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h prints some orderly continuation attempts.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the concept is still anti-chop there.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Sparse and smaller continuation carry than TAO.

long: Next bar low stays >= EMA_20 - 0.08 * ATR_14
short: Next bar high stays <= EMA_20 + 0.08 * ATR_14

long: Trail remaining 60% by 0.82 * ATR_14 while close remains >= EMA_55
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% after 6 bars if no 1.0 ATR follow-through
short: Trail remaining 60% by 0.82 * ATR_14 while close remains <= EMA_55
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% after 6 bars if no 1.0 ATR follow-through
